claim: UNAIDS reports 39 million PLHIV globally, 25 million in SSA. 86% know status, 76% on treatment, 71% virally suppressed. Still short of 95-95-95 targets with largest gaps in western and central Africa.

source.title: The Urgency of Now: AIDS at a Crossroads - UNAIDS Global AIDS Update 2024

method: Reviewed UNAIDS report cascade data, regional breakdowns, and 95-95-95 tracking.
limitations: Cascade estimates rely on modeled inputs where data is incomplete.
Sub-national cascade data is not systematically reported.

claim: PHIA surveys provide gold-standard population-based HIV cascade estimates at district level with superior accuracy to routine program data, but are conducted every 3-5 years limiting temporal resolution.

source.title: Population-based HIV Impact Assessments: methods, findings, and future directions

method: Reviewed PHIA methodology, cascade estimation approach, and comparison to routine data.
limitations: PHIA surveys cover a limited number of countries.
Survey frequency limits temporal resolution for program monitoring.
